tool_args.raw_text: proposed service guideline-based changes and the proposed two-year wage freeze are removed from this Plan, eliminating savings of almost $1.4 billion through 2025 that had been included in the July Plan

payload.description: Savings from service guideline changes and two-year wage freeze removed from November Plan

tool_args.label: Structural Imbalance Statement
tool_args.raw_text: the federal funding, however, masks the structural imbalance in MTA's finances. Without the fare and toll increases, ARPA and the use of deficit borrowing proceeds, 2025 would be $3.2 billion out of balance. Even if the fare and toll increases were implemented, 2025 would still be out of balance by $2.4 billion without federal funding and the deficit borrowing proceeds. And with federal funding exhausted in 2025 and only $1.5 billion remaining in deficit borrowing proceeds, 2026 will likely be significantly out of balance without actions to address the structural imbalance.

payload.claim_text: the federal funding, however, masks the structural imbalance in MTA's finances. Without the fare and toll increases, ARPA and the use of deficit borrowing proceeds, 2025 would be $3.2 billion out of balance. Even if the fare and toll increases were implemented, 2025 would still be out of balance by $2.4 billion without federal funding and the deficit borrowing proceeds. And with federal funding exhausted in 2025 and only $1.5 billion remaining in deficit borrowing proceeds, 2026 will likely be significantly out of balance without actions to address the structural imbalance.
payload.description: Statement that federal funding masks a structural imbalance in MTA finances

tool_args.label: Service at 100% Pre-Pandemic
tool_args.raw_text: Currently, subway and bus service are scheduled at 100 percent of pre-pandemic levels with expectations of meeting that schedule daily, while the LIRR is providing service at approximately 85 percent of its pre-pandemic level and MNR is providing approximately 82 percent of pre-pandemic service.

payload.claim_text: subway and bus service are scheduled at 100 percent of pre-pandemic levels with expectations of meeting that schedule daily, while the LIRR is providing service at approximately 85 percent of its pre-pandemic level and MNR is providing approximately 82 percent of pre-pandemic service
payload.description: Current service levels as percent of pre-pandemic

tool_args.label: Central Business District Tolling Program
tool_args.raw_text: Central Business District Tolling Program (CBDTP), which aims to reduce congestion in Manhattan's Central Business District (CBD) and, along with other revenues in the Capital Lockbox, will generate the revenue necessary to support $15 billion in bonding capacity for the 2020-2024 and subsequent MTA Capital Programs

payload.description: Aims to reduce congestion in Manhattan's Central Business District and generate revenue to support $15 billion in bonding capacity for MTA Capital Programs

tool_args.label: Federal funding masks structural imbalance
tool_args.raw_text: the federal funding, however, masks the structural imbalance in MTA's finances. Without the fare and toll increases, ARPA and the use of deficit borrowing proceeds, 2025 would be $3.2 billion out of balance. Even if the fare and toll increases were implemented, 2025 would still be out of balance by $2.4 billion without federal funding and the deficit borrowing proceeds. And with federal funding exhausted in 2025 and only $1.5 billion remaining in deficit borrowing proceeds, 2026 will likely be significantly out of balance without actions to address the structural imbalance.

payload.gap_text: the federal funding, however, masks the structural imbalance in MTA's finances. Without the fare and toll increases, ARPA and the use of deficit borrowing proceeds, 2025 would be $3.2 billion out of balance
payload.missing_information: Sustainable funding sources to address structural imbalance beyond 2025
payload.description: The Plan is balanced only through temporary federal funding and deficit borrowing; a structural imbalance remains for 2026 and beyond

tool_args.label: Balancing contingent on fare/toll increases and borrowing
tool_args.raw_text: While the Plan is balanced through 2025, it is only balanced with $10.5 billion in federal funding through CRRSAA and ARPA, as well as with the $4.0 billion in CARES Act funding that was received and used to balance the 2020 budget. The Plan's balanced bottom line is also contingent on the implementation of proposed fare and toll increases in 2022, 2023 and 2025, which contribute a total of $1.8 billion in revenue over the course of the Plan period. In 2025, almost $1.4 billion of proceeds from deficit borrowing will be needed to close that year's budget gap.

payload.gap_text: the Plan is balanced through 2025, it is only balanced with $10.5 billion in federal funding...contingent on the implementation of proposed fare and toll increases
payload.missing_information: Whether fare/toll increases will be approved and whether additional federal funding will be available
payload.description: The balanced budget is contingent on fare/toll increases, federal funding, and deficit borrowing
